utilityName: City of Sacramento Department of Utilities Cross-Connection Control
testingFrequency: At installation and on the local recurring certification cycle
dueBasis: Sacramento's city and county cross-connection workflow uses approved tester lists and a county portal for registered testers and electronic test entry. The city's water certification language applies up to the property line, backflow preventer, or water meter.
coveredPropertyTypes: Protected services in Sacramento's water service area
officialListLabel: Open the Sacramento approved backflow tester list

submissionMethods.label: Sacramento County cross connection tester registry

penalties: Sacramento keeps cross-connection control inside the regulated drinking-water boundary and relies on approved or registered testers for accepted submissions. Missing the recognized workflow can leave the assembly outside the accepted local compliance path.
sourceExcerpt: Sacramento is a strong California city because the Department of Utilities exposes a real tester list and the county portal maintains registered testers and electronic test-entry operations for local compliance.
verdictSummary: Sacramento is a useful California utility because city drinking-water pages and county cross-connection operations meet in an actual approved-tester workflow.
whoIsAffected: City of Sacramento water customers and protected services that must stay inside Sacramento's cross-connection control and registered tester workflow.
residentialNotes: Residential demand is strongest when tied to irrigation or another protected service rather than generic city-water questions.
The approved tester list gives homeowners with protected devices a concrete next action.
commercialNotes: Commercial value is strong because Sacramento publishes an approved tester list and relies on the county's active tester-registration system.
This is the kind of utility/county split users struggle with, which makes the page commercially useful.

annualTesting.summary: Sacramento uses approved or registered testers and county-run electronic data-entry operations to keep local backflow certifications current.
highlights: The city publishes a local approved tester list.
Sacramento County maintains registered testers and electronic test-result entry.
Drinking-water certification language explicitly references the backflow preventer boundary.
workflowSteps: Confirm the device is inside Sacramento's protected service workflow.
Use an approved or registered local tester.
Keep the city-county reporting chain current so the certification is accepted locally.

irrigation.summary: Sacramento irrigation demand fits because the city and county keep cross-connection control tied to real service boundaries and registered testers.
highlights: Irrigation is a common trigger for protected service-connection testing.
County registration and test entry make the process more operational than generic city copy.
workflowSteps: Confirm the irrigation assembly needs local certification.
Choose a tester from the approved list.
Make sure the result gets into the local reporting workflow.

fireLine.summary: Sacramento is less explicit about fire-line rules than some Texas cities, but the approved-tester and protected-boundary workflow still supports larger protected services.
highlights: The city's protected-water boundary runs to the backflow preventer or meter.
The county registry is built for recurring compliance, not one-off plumbing work.
workflowSteps: Confirm the service connection class and local requirement.
Use an approved tester familiar with the assembly type.
Keep the local city-county filing path complete.
Confirm the service falls under Sacramento's cross-connection workflow.
Use a tester from the city-approved list.
Complete the required certification or retest.
Keep the tester registry and local filing path current so the report is accepted.
failureHighlights: Sacramento publishes a real approved tester list.
County registration and test entry create a live operations layer behind the city page.
The city's water-quality pages explicitly mention the backflow preventer boundary.

costBand.testingRange: Testing is market-priced, but Sacramento narrows the path through an approved tester list.
costBand.repairRetestRange: Repair and retest cost depends on assembly class and how much follow-up is needed in the county portal workflow.
costBand.pricingNotes: The main local value is clarity around the city-county compliance chain, not a posted retail fee.
